prohibited.label: AI prohibited
prohibited.description: Generative AI use in court filings is outright prohibited.

prohibited_except_assisted_research.label: AI prohibited except approved legal-research tools
prohibited_except_assisted_research.description: Generative AI prohibited; traditional/approved legal-research tools (e.g., Lexis, Westlaw, Bloomberg Law) permitted.

disclosure_required.label: AI permitted with disclosure / certification
disclosure_required.description: AI use permitted but counsel must disclose use and/or certify human verification.

disclosure_except_assisted_research.label: Disclosure required except for approved legal-research tools
disclosure_except_assisted_research.description: Disclosure required for generative AI; approved legal-research tools exempted from disclosure obligation.

permitted_with_caution.label: AI permitted with general accuracy obligation
permitted_with_caution.description: Order acknowledges AI use, imposes general accuracy/competence obligation, no affirmative disclosure duty.

no_explicit_rule.label: No explicit AI rule
no_explicit_rule.description: Court or judge has standing orders or local rules but none specifically address AI.

permitted.label: AI affirmatively permitted
permitted.description: Order or rule affirmatively permits AI use without disclosure or special conditions beyond default professional-conduct rules.

rules.court: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Texas
rules.title: Mandatory Certification Regarding Generative Artificial Intelligence
rules.summary: Requires every attorney appearing before the court to file a certification that either (a) no portion of any filing was drafted by generative AI, or (b) any language drafted by generative AI was checked for accuracy by a human being using print reporters or traditional legal databases.
rules.provenance: hand-seeded; widely reported as one of the first U.S. judicial AI standing orders; verbatim language and current status pending agent verification on first run

rules.title: Order on Artificial Intelligence
rules.summary: Requires parties using generative AI to draft any portion of a filing to disclose the program used and the specific portions drafted by AI, and to certify human verification of citations and legal analysis.
rules.provenance: hand-seeded; verbatim language and current status pending agent verification on first run

rules.court: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Illinois
rules.title: Standing Order for Civil Cases Before Magistrate Judge Fuentes
rules.summary: Requires disclosure in any filing of any use of generative AI, identification of the AI tool used, and certification that a human has checked the accuracy of any AI-generated content including citations.
rules.provenance: hand-seeded; verbatim language and current status pending agent verification on first run

rules.court: U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Pennsylvania
rules.title: Standing Order Re: Use of Artificial Intelligence
rules.summary: Requires counsel to disclose any use of AI in preparing a filing and to certify that the accuracy of all citations and legal arguments has been verified.
rules.provenance: hand-seeded; verbatim language and current status pending agent verification on first run

rules.court: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit
rules.title: Proposed amendment requiring AI certification (proposed Rule 32.3 / Form 6 amendment)
rules.summary: The Fifth Circuit proposed an amendment to its local rules requiring counsel to certify either that no generative AI was used in drafting briefs or that any AI-generated text and citations were reviewed for accuracy by a human. Public comment period followed; status of final adoption pending agent verification.
rules.provenance: hand-seeded; this entry was a *proposal* and may have been withdrawn, modified, or adopted; status pending agent verification on first run
